author: @Gainn
text: Use a pair of mountain bike wheels on the back?

author: @BoB4jjjjs
text: You don't need to tell me about the price of wheelchairs or electric scooters, they are terrible!  Even second hand they are not cheap!

author: @giovannip.1433
text: The width is about right as well due to their design for moving down isles and access ways.

author: @pgprentice
text: I still want to know who the MORON was that decided it was a great idea to put 4 castors on a shopping cart?  Only the UK and Australia have these dumb carts.   The rest of the world has only the front wheels as castors and the back ones fixed.  Thanks for another great video.

replies.author: @Earzone63
replies.text: Probably because they were invented here by someone smart before they were refined — many zero to one inventions are like that, and afterwards it becomes cost prohibitive/ unnecessary / slow to replace e.g. extreme hot/cold plumbing vs mixer taps.

author: @gazzaka
text: Seems a slight danger of looking like a hillbilly !

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: more than slight I would suggest lol

author: @Patriarchtech
text: Why am I paying for this Robert? Like it is a noble thing to help the disabled and it is our responsibility. This channel though is reaching the point of being silly.  I really want to be positive here.....But most of the things you do is at best a tangent to being something useful in any application.

replies.author: @401ksolar
replies.text: There are two reason somebody would pay, one is because you are interested in the subject matter number two is you are trying to control the subject matter?

replies.author: @Patriarchtech
replies.text: No I like Robert, Like his attitude and his blunt approach. Also I love the experiments.  But that does not mean that I cannot criticize. I would never try to control the subject matter, I love creativity. But that is not the same as saying all his Ideas inspires forward movement/progression. Certainly  it is fun to watch but is it useful?

replies.author: @401ksolar
replies.text: @Patriarchtech yes there are comments in here from people who are looking to have a Mobility option that they can actually afford it's nice to be so rich where you can afford whatever you want but not everyone has that option

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: If you are feeling I am not addressing your needs or providing you with the value you need or want the you do right to raise the issue. However, value for you is not the same as value for others and here you are on the public channel - I try to provide value for members through the members channel which I would say was far from silly - if there are things you would like to see on the members channel I am more than happy to address them there - but here - it is a wide audience and people like silly sometimes as well as not silly and to be honest I would think not every one gets what they want - But seriously - thank you for pointing it out and if there is something you want me to look at please do let me know and I will do it - but only on the members channel I am afraid - cheers

replies.author: @lisataylor3085
replies.text: Anders, I don't see it as being silly. We will all get old. We can't all afford 10 grand for an electric wheelchair.

author: @Twistedmetal-qe8kx
text: Ohh yeah, there's going to be a motor!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: oh yeah!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

replies.author: @chuxxsss
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I don't need it for anything.

author: @gaylanward5424
text: My wheelchair cost more than $10,000.00 US and it is not even comfortable!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: good lord! - everyone who has sat in the one I made didn't want to get out lol

author: @stevetobias4890
text:
The steering will be interesting.
Love it, thanks Rob.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - that's for sure

author: @deslomeslager
text:
Hi Rob. I have lost the reason for why you want to motorize everything. Maybe it's the age? Because I am 54 but I really don't want to look at e-bikes. Not in it's normal form, as you know I use my own e-bike to create energy. Or is it so that the younger generation only wants things to be motorized? I do know we live in the age of Aquarius (joking). I mean in the era of electrifying everything. But after having shown to us several times you _can_ do it, we believe it. (I do).
Any how. Keep up playing with wheels. 
PS: yesterday I left you a comment (with a YT link) on your previous video, the one about the iron battery. It may become 'big' in the near future (pun intended).

replies.author: @Teknopottu
replies.text: Not all of us younglings want to see everything motorised but those that do, find it very inspiring if not hugely entertaining. Perhaps this goes with children of all ages, some like motorised toys more and for longer time than others.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I do two things mate - the main youtube is mostly about entertainment if truth be told. The videos that get watched are just the entertaining ones all those vids with awesome info hardly get viewed - I tend , these days, to do the more serious information containing stuff on the members channel - there really is a distinct difference between the two - I saw the iron air battery stuff - it may become 'big' or it may be just another 'fad' - time will tell

author: @ericvonessex9353
text: Excellent idea, there are often cheaper and better ways of getting around prohibitively expensive items. Changing the subject, an idea for a future show is if someone can come up with an idea of a motorized gadget to clean and wash the inside and outside of a gutter! I have two problems firstly I can't stand heights and secondly a conservatory that prevents ladder access to a gutter. I'm currently designing a motorized gizmo that can be placed on the side of the small area of gutter that is accessible. There's one heck of a lot of design problems to overcome.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that is a good idea mate - and I can 'see' what you mean about design problems - good luck with it

replies.author: @ericvonessex9353
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Many thanks.

author: @robertpitt8418
text: Putting one's nanny in a shopping trolley with a built-in rocket motor is probably not a good idea -or is it   : )

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol

author: @RaperJason
text:
Best laugh I've had in ages mate 🤣 Briliant idea for upcycling 🤣 but with my sence of humour I can now see Granny in a run away trolly with the wonky wheel that is always on the trolly I pick screaming down a steep hill with no brakes and family in hot persuit 🤣
And when it gets to the grid that stops you stealing the trolly from sainsburys it stops dead throwing her on the floor 🤣 or she goes across the car park into the line of trollies and gets stacked 🤣 If keep up this level good work you could put Dignitas and Exit out of business 🤣

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - you hit my funny bone too mate - that is hilarious!

author: @StrumHollow
text: I knew that kid's bikes would be a gateway in crime. Now you're nicking shopping trolleys? :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - no - you can buy them you know. Alternatively, here in the UK if you find abandoned property you need to publicly advertise it and allow the owner 6 months to turn up and reclaim it, or you can find the previous owner and ask them if you can have it - after that it's yours. This particular shopping trolly came from a supermarket here in Canterbury that closed down 5 years ago. I found it in the river and cleaned it up

replies.author: @StrumHollow
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Good on ya! Cleaning up the waterways & repurposing.

author: @kilokilos
text: Hey good one and shopping trollies are going at an exceptionally good price in KZN right now. Newspapers on the other hand is like reinforced toilet paper, mostly I think people only have iPads and the like around nowdays, not reading a lot in ye older slave colony.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: awesome lol

author: @lisataylor3085
text: Wow! you actually read the comments of your videos and take ideas from them. Thank you for doing this. I hope it will be able to go off road. My 82 year old mother wants. to go rockhounding and currently there is no affordable option for her.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @joohop
text:
Looks Ace !
Bless Up Fella

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @davewesley5048
text: Can see Tesco’s losing even more trollies than normal

replies.author: @lisataylor3085
replies.text: There is no reason to steal anything in this world. Ask the manager if Tesco and others have any for sale. Many times, they'll just give you one of the damaged ones.

replies.author: @matthewellisor5835
replies.text:
@lisataylor3085 Right-on!
Before I had kids, I once took back one I had found in a ditch and, on cleaning it up could see a store number. 
The manager thanked me and asked if I was scrapping as a side income. I answered yes but that I was more interested in the wheels and frame. I ended up taking 3 old ones there and she called ahead and sent me to the other store in town for two more.
It pays off to be good to one another.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Lisa and Mathew are right mate - just ask - equally eBay has second hand ones for sale at around UK£30

author: @frankendoll1455
text: The things that go through your mind! Can't wait to see the motorized version!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - cheers mate

author: @Earzone63
text:
I'm imagining there's at least a chance that two videos time this will have some helicopter contraption bolted over head ... "I was wondering why wheelchair users should have to use their hands to propel themselves forward" 🤣 
Mate your ingenuity never seizes to amaze me :D

replies.author: @lisataylor3085
replies.text: I agree. I've been waiting for him to do his version of those old kits on using a VW engine and making a gyrocopter.

replies.author: @matthewellisor5835
replies.text:
@lisataylor3085 Right there with you. I've wanted to build an autogyro for ages but homeschooling two kids, 40+hours/wk and a run-down house are keeping me busy right now. 
But I'll start on it before I next take vacation/holiday. :D I have been collecting parts.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - cheers - you know I have been wanting to do something like that for ages myself lol
